messages.content:
Mathematics Distillation Challenge - Equational Theories: Stage 2 is now live

Hi everyone,

Stage 2 of the Mathematics Distillation Challenge - Equational Theories is now live.

At a high level, Stage 2 is an autoformalization challenge: participants will build systems that translate equational reasoning into Lean 4 artifacts that can be checked automatically by a deterministic judge.

This stage moves from true/false prediction to machine-checkable Lean 4 certificates. For each equational implication, submissions must provide either a Lean proof that the implication is true, or a Lean certificate of a counterexample showing that the implication is false.

Stage 2 has two tracks:

Solo: one solver subprocess per problem.
Marathon: one solver subprocess handles a batch of problems under a shared global budget.

Both tracks use the same deterministic Lean judge and the same public verdict statuses. Submissions should be a single solver.py file, up to 500 KB.

The official repository includes the evaluation pipeline, Lean judge, demo solvers, tutorials, and local testing tools.

Stage 2 is open to everyone.

Deadline: August 31, 2026, 23:59 AoE

Competition page: https://competition.sair.foundation/competitions/mathematics-distillation-challenge-equational-theories-stage2/overview

Playground: https://playground.sair.foundation/playground/mathematics-distillation-challenge-equational-theories-stage2

Please note that the Stage 2 Playground is currently available only to participants who registered for Stage 1.

The Stage 2 Contributor Network will open soon.

Repository: https://github.com/SAIRcompetition/equational-theories-lean-stage2

If you run into setup problems, judge bugs, unclear documentation, or reproducible errors, please open a GitHub issue in the official repository. Feedback on the rules and evaluation setup is also welcome.

Thank you for participating in SAIR competitions! Good luck, and we’re excited to see what the community builds.
messages.subject: Stage 2 is Live!
